sanskrit:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढा: सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु।
तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत्॥
transliteration: prakṛter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu | tān akṛtsna-vido mandān kṛtsna-vin na vicālayet ||
literal_meaning: Deluded by the guṇas of prakṛti, they cling to guna-actions; the complete-knower should not disturb the slow/incomplete-knowers.
contextual_meaning: People hypnotized by their personality traits mistake role-based busyness for identity; the self-realized quietly model wholeness rather than debating the hypnotized.
psychological_state: Secure non-attachment that respects each psyche’s timetable; refrains from rescuing or proselytizing.
trigger_scenario: A mindfulness coach wants to correct coworkers gossiping at the water-cooler; instead of preaching, she breathes and returns to her mat, trusting their curiosity to awaken when ready.
anchor_text: Let the wise embody peace, not argue with those still tangled in their traits.
